(00:13:12) Nicholas J. Fuentes discusses recent developments where [[Donald Trump]] appeared to negotiate a deal on [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]] with [[Democratic Party|Democratic]] leaders [[Nancy Pelosi]] and [[Chuck Schumer]].

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:14:00) Nicholas J. Fuentes quotes a tweet from [[Donald Trump]] stating, "No deal was made last night on [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]]" and that any agreement would require "massive border security."

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:14:17) Nicholas J. Fuentes quotes a subsequent tweet from [[Donald Trump]] expressing sympathy for "good, educated, and accomplished young people" brought to the [[United States]] illegally as children.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:14:46) Nicholas J. Fuentes states that figures like [[Ann Coulter]] and [[Sean Hannity]] criticized [[Donald Trump]] over his [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]] tweets, with [[Ann Coulter]] even calling for his impeachment.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:15:06) Nicholas J. Fuentes claims that prominent figures from the "alt-right" and conservative media, including [[Richard Spencer]] and [[Theodore Robert Beale|Vox Day]], came out against [[Donald Trump]] on the [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]] issue.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:15:34) Nicholas J. Fuentes analyzes [[Donald Trump]]'s strategy by comparing the [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]] situation to the [[United States|U.S]]. missile strike on [[Syria]] in April, which he argues was a limited show of force aimed at [[China]] and [[North Korea]], not the start of a war.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:16:01) Nicholas J. Fuentes references an alleged chemical weapons attack in [[Syria]] that prompted the [[United States|U.S]]. strike, noting that figures like [[Nikki Haley]] and [[Rex Tillerson]] had previously stated [[United States|U.S]]. policy was not to remove [[Bashar al-Assad]].

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:16:45) Nicholas J. Fuentes states that [[Bashar al-Assad]] was winning the [[Syria|Syrian]] [[civil war]] with the help of [[Vladimir Putin]] before the alleged chemical attack.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:22:51) Nicholas J. Fuentes notes that [[Donald Trump]] removed [[Steven Crowder|Steve Bannon]] and [[Sebastian Gorka]] from the [[White House]] earlier in the month, with rumors of [[Steven Crowder|Steve Bannon]] returning to lead [[Breitbart News|Breitbart]].

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:23:15) Nicholas J. Fuentes mentions that [[Fox News]] chose [[Laura Ingraham]] to replace [[Bill O'Reilly]], which he interprets as a move to counter [[Steven Crowder|Steve Bannon]]'s potential media network.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:23:39) Nicholas J. Fuentes argues that [[Donald Trump]]'s [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]] negotiations with [[Democratic Party|Democrats]] are a tactic to pressure [[Paul Ryan]] and [[Mitch McConnell]] into offering wall funding to avoid a deal that would hurt the [[Republican Party]].

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:25:00) Nicholas J. Fuentes predicts that [[Paul Ryan]] and [[Mitch McConnell]] will come to [[Donald Trump]] with a counteroffer to fund the border wall in exchange for some form of [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]] resolution.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:26:22) Nicholas J. Fuentes cites [[Donald Trump]]'s book *The Art of the Deal*, specifically the "[[Donald Trump|Trump]] cards" chapter, to support his theory that [[the President]] uses bold promises and strategic deals to achieve his goals.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:28:04) Nicholas J. Fuentes clarifies [[Donald Trump]]'s campaign promises on immigration, stating [[the President]] pledged to deport criminal illegal aliens, build a wall, and then "a determination will be made as to the rest."

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:32:19) Nicholas J. Fuentes references commentators [[Mike Cernovich]] and [[Jack Posobiec]], [[who]] he claims were wrong in predicting the [[Syria]] missile strike would lead to a major war.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:33:09) Nicholas J. Fuentes reiterates his view that the [[Syria]] strike was a "show of force to [[China]] and [[North Korea]]," timed before a visit from [[Xi Jinping]] to Mar-a-Lago.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:35:51) Nicholas J. Fuentes argues that [[Donald Trump]]'s [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]] tweets were intended to "rile up his base" and generate pressure on [[Congress]], pointing to [[Ann Coulter]]'s critical reaction as part of the plan.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:38:10) Nicholas J. Fuentes responds to a viewer question about demographic change, stating the [[United States]] shifted from 90% white in 1960 to 67% white in 2017, a process he describes as active and intentional.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:40:16) Nicholas J. Fuentes contrasts his views with [[Ben Shapiro]], stating, "[[Ben Shapiro]] sees no problem with this" demographic shift, while arguing that nations like [[France]] and [[Germany]] should be for their native ethnicities.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:41:36) Nicholas J. Fuentes argues that wanting a [[country]] to remain ethnically homogeneous is considered normal in nations like [[Iran]], [[China]], and [[Nigeria]], but is vilified as "white nationalism" in [[Europe]] and the [[United States]].

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:44:47) Nicholas J. Fuentes recounts seeing a diversity mural at [[Boston University]] that included a [[Islam|Muslim]] woman in a hijab, a group he estimates represents less than half a percent of the [[United States|U.S]]. population, questioning the agenda behind such representation.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:47:21) Nicholas J. Fuentes claims that "globalists" pursue demographic change to create a "disassociated slave class" of individual consumers without national, familial, or communal ties, making them easier to control.

(00:50:53) Nicholas J. Fuentes comments on a controversial rap video depicting the hanging of a white child, suggesting the artist's intent was to criticize [[Black Lives Matter]] hypocrisy, but the imagery would be exploited to radicalize people.

(00:54:30) Nicholas J. Fuentes responds to a question about what [[Donald Trump]] could do if threatened by the "powers that be," stating [[the President]] would need a loyal "praetorian guard" to prevent assassination or poisoning.

(00:55:47) Nicholas J. Fuentes criticizes the recent *Spider-Man* movie for including "Social Justice Warrior garbage," such as a line about the [[Washington Monument]] being built by slaves and a non-canonical [[black]] love interest for Spider-Man.

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(00:58:48) Nicholas J. Fuentes expresses support for [[Donald Trump]]'s tax reform agenda, arguing that growing the middle-class economy enables family formation, and states he has moved away from supporting a flat tax toward a progressive system based on marginal utility.

(01:03:17) Nicholas J. Fuentes comments on the imprisonment of [[Martin Shkreli]], linking it to the cases of [[Margo Grell]], [[Chris Cantwell]], and [[Ursula Haverbeck]], whom he says are jailed for speech, proving the [[United States]] is not "the freest [[country]] in the world."

### AF 2017-09-14 0010
(01:04:28) Nicholas J. Fuentes criticizes [[Ben Shapiro]] and [[Dave Rubin]] as hypocrites on free speech, noting they do not defend figures like [[Richard Spencer]], [[Jared Taylor]], or [[Theodore Robert Beale|Vox Day]], or speak against laws criminalizing [[United States Holocaust Memorial Museum|Holocaust]] denial in countries like [[France]].

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:00:00) Nicholas J. Fuentes hosted a debate on the [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] program, titled "[[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals|DACA]] Demolition," featuring guest [[Will Nardi]].

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:03:35) Nicholas J. Fuentes argued that the primary responsibility of the [[United States]] government is to protect the life, liberty, and property of the [[United States|American]] people, not the welfare of foreign children.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:07:24) Nicholas J. Fuentes countered that Article 1, Section 8 of the [[U.S. Constitution|United States Constitution]] authorizes spending taxpayer money to enforce laws, including border laws, which he argued should be a higher priority than [[Health and Human Services|Medicare]] or [[Social Security]].

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:08:39) Nicholas J. Fuentes argued that the cost of deporting an illegal immigrant is less than the cost of educating a pupil in the [[Chicagoland]] area, citing the high fertility rates of foreign-born Mexican women as a long-term fiscal burden.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:12:10) Nicholas J. Fuentes clarified that his core argument for deportation was not fiscal but based on the principle that the government must enforce its laws and prioritize the interests of [[United States|American]] citizens over non-citizens.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:13:17) Nicholas J. Fuentes argued that the median age of [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] recipients is 25, meaning many are still consuming public education resources, which represents a net fiscal drain.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:16:13) Nicholas J. Fuentes argued that the social contract and public benefits in the [[United States]] are built on the contributions of generations of [[United States|American]] taxpayers and their ancestors, not on those [[who]] entered the [[country]] illegally.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:24:46) Nicholas J. Fuentes cited the case of [[Emanuel Rangel]], a [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] recipient and gang member [[who]] was allowed to stay in the [[United States]] and later murdered four [[United States|American]] citizens, as an example of preventable crime caused by failed border enforcement.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:26:46) Nicholas J. Fuentes stated that 93% of [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] recipients are [[Hispanic]], with the vast majority coming from [[Mexico]] and other broken countries rather than active war zones.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:31:47) Nicholas J. Fuentes argued that while domestic crime is inherent to a free society, every crime committed by an illegal immigrant is 100% preventable through strict border enforcement and deportation.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:35:15) [[Will Nardi]] challenged Nicholas J. Fuentes's [[Christianity|Christian]] faith, arguing that a policy of deportation without forgiveness was hypocritical and contradicted [[Christianity|Christian]] values of compassion.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:36:18) Nicholas J. Fuentes stated that he understood why individuals from broken countries like [[Mexico]] would break the law to enter the [[United States]] for a better life and did not begrudge them for it.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:37:07) Nicholas J. Fuentes argued that the failure of foreign societies does not obligate the [[United States]] to "pick up the slack," and that the [[United States|American government]]'s primary duty is to its 300 million citizens [[who]] pay taxes and have historical ties to the nation.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:38:50) Nicholas J. Fuentes stated that 50% of [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] recipients live in [[State of California|California]] or [[State of Texas|Texas]], and that those not living in heavily affected states may not bear the brunt of the policy's adverse effects.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:45:32) Nicholas J. Fuentes stated that [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] stands for "[[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]]," emphasizing that "deferred" does not mean permanent and that recipients understood the temporary nature of their status when they arrived.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:46:30) Nicholas J. Fuentes cited [[The Huffington Post|Huffington Post]] and [[Amnesty International]] statistics indicating that 60-80% of women are raped while crossing the southern border illegally, arguing that allowing [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] recipients to stay creates a moral hazard that incentivizes this dangerous journey.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:47:48) Nicholas J. Fuentes argued that deporting illegal immigrants and closing borders is the most compassionate policy, as it prevents a dangerous journey, stops a "brain drain" from source countries, and allows the best and brightest to improve their native nations.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:50:37) Nicholas J. Fuentes noted that the [[DREAM Act]] was shot down in both the [[House]] and [[Senate]], and that the [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] program was created unconstitutionally by executive fiat under [[White House|President]] [[Barack Obama]].

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:51:11) Nicholas J. Fuentes stated that [[Donald Trump]] was elected [[president]] on a platform that included rescinding [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] on his first day in office, representing the will of the [[United States|American]] people.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:55:56) Nicholas J. Fuentes cited [[Pew Research Center|Pew Research]] data showing that 66% of [[Hispanic|Hispanics]] voted for [[Hillary Clinton]] in 2016, 68% opposed the border wall, and 78% supported amnesty for illegal immigrants, arguing that [[Democratic Party|Democrats]] support [[Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals]] for political gain.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(00:56:56) Nicholas J. Fuentes cited further [[Pew Research Center|Pew Research]] data indicating that 75% of [[Hispanic|Hispanics]] support big government, compared to 41% of the general public, arguing that both political parties support illegal immigration for cheap labor and future votes, not out of compassion.

### AF 2017-09-13 0009
(01:00:03) Nicholas J. Fuentes concluded that both major political parties have pushed for third-world immigration since the 1965 [[Hart-Celler Act]], arguing that the incoming demographic broadly supports bigger government and policies contrary to the nation's founding principles, serving a cosmopolitan elite at the expense of the [[United States|American]] people.
